front.title: IPv6 Path Performance Degradation in Dual-Stack Networks

abstract.t: The document analyzes contributing factors across the content service layer and the network transport layer, and discusses why existing mechanisms such as RFC 6724 and Happy Eyeballs do not fully address performance failures that appear after connection establishment. This document is limited to problem analysis, scope clarification, and areas for further study. It does not define new network-to-host signaling mechanisms or require hosts to use network-provided information when making address-family or path selection decisions.
